name: PR-CC 79 Ruta de los Pontones
description:
Route PR-CC 79 Los Pontones is a circular route that departs from the Tejeda de Tiétar bus stop in the Tiétar Valley, Cáceres. You can access it by public transport. The route follows well-marked trails, paths, and paths through the dehesa, gorges, and viewpoints.

You can do it in the opposite direction to the recommended route. Don't forget to visit the Chorrera de Matagarcía and enjoy the scenery among holm oaks, rockroses, and cattle. Depending on the time of year you take the circular route, you'll appreciate the vivid colors of autumn or the greenery that stands out in spring. We recommend both seasons.

After crossing the Garguera Gorge and reaching the waterfall, return to the main path to enjoy the natural surroundings: stone pontoons, waterways, and the banks of the gorges. On the final stretch, back to the village, you can visit the 16th-century Church of San Miguel, which houses the curious anthropomorphic tombstone called "La Muerte Pelona" (Bald Death), and the pillory known as "Micaela" in the main square.

This route combines heritage, water, and landscapes, making it ideal for autumn or spring, where you can enjoy both nature and local history. Don't forget to pack plenty of water and snacks to replenish your energy.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails Plasencia offer diverse natural landscapes, including river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and gentle hills. The region is characterized by the Jerte Valley, known for its picturesque scenery, and the nearby Monfragüe National Park. The Tagus River winds through the area, providing natural routes and contributing to the local biodiversity. These features create varied terrain suitable for accessible walks and short excursions. Best easy hiking trails around Plasencia The most popular easy hiking route is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route features scenic waterfalls and offers views of the Jerte Valley. Another top favourite among local hikers is Los Llanos - Calderón Trail, an easy 3.4 miles (5.5 km) path. This trail explores the natural surroundings with gentle elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Via de la Plata - ride around the walled city of Galisteo, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through historic landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around Plasencia is defined by river valleys, Mediterranean forests, and historic paths like the Vía de la Plata. The network offers options for different ability levels, focusing on accessible and enjoyable walks. The routes in Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available around Plasencia?
A: Plasencia offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 40 routes specifically categorized as easy. In total, the region boasts more than 120 hiking trails, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy trails with waterfalls near Plasencia?
A: Yes, the region is known for its beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990319686/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular easy route that features scenic waterfalls and offers stunning views of the Jerte Valley. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogaleas Waterfalls – Suspension bridge of the waterfall loop from Navaconcejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Which easy trails are suitable for families with children?
A: Many easy trails around Plasencia are suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Park – Isla Park loop from Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle route within the city, perfect for a relaxed family stroll. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10371348" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Jerte – Nogaleas Waterfalls loop from Navaconcejo&lt;/a&gt; is another shorter, easy option that provides beautiful natural scenery.

Q: Can I find circular easy walks in the Plasencia area?
A: Absolutely. Many easy trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Valle del Jerte – Jerte River loop from Jerte&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21950965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia – Plasencia Reservoir loop from Río Jerte&lt;/a&gt;, both offering pleasant loops through natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any easy trails where I can spot wildlife?
A: The natural landscapes around Plasencia, especially areas like Monfragüe National Park (a short distance away), are excellent for wildlife spotting. While specific easy trails are not exclusively designated for wildlife, routes through the Jerte Valley or along the Tagus River, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990729084/los-llanos-calderon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Llanos - Calderón Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer opportunities to observe local birds and other small animals in their natural habitat.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see on easy hikes?
A: The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990869677/via-de-la-plata-ride-around-the-walled-city-of-galisteo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via de la Plata - ride around the walled city of Galisteo&lt;/a&gt; follows a historic Roman road, offering a glimpse into the past. You can also explore areas near castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5679059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre Lucía of Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, which are often accessible via nearby trails or short walks.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy trails in Plasencia?
A: The easy trails around Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers frequently praise the diverse natural landscapes, including the picturesque river valleys and Mediterranean forests, as well as the well-maintained paths that make for enjoyable and accessible walks.

Q: When is the best time of year for easy hikes around Plasencia?
A: Plasencia offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring is particularly beautiful, especially in the Jerte Valley during the cherry blossom season. Autumn also provides mild temperatures and vibrant foliage. Even winter can be suitable for easy walks, though checking local weather conditions is always advisable.

Q: Are there easy trails near the Plasencia Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21950965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Plasencia – Plasencia Reservoir loop from Río Jerte&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, scenic route that takes you around the reservoir, offering tranquil views and a gentle elevation profile. It's a great option for a relaxed walk near the water.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in Plasencia?
A: Easy hikes around Plasencia traverse diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter lush river valleys, such as the Jerte Valley, known for its cherry trees and waterfalls. Many trails also wind through Mediterranean forests and gentle hills, offering a mix of shaded paths and open vistas. The Tagus River also contributes to the area's rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there easy trails that follow the Jerte River?
A: Yes, the Jerte River is a prominent feature of the region, and several easy trails follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17315121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camping Valle del Jerte – Jerte River loop from Jerte&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38221142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Jerte – Jerte River loop from Cabezuela del Valle&lt;/a&gt; are excellent examples, providing serene riverside walks with minimal elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy trails within Plasencia city limits?
A: Within Plasencia itself, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19433165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isla Park&lt;/a&gt; offers a large municipal park along the Jerte River. It features a serene riverside promenade that is ideal for easy walking and relaxing, providing a natural escape right in the city.
tips: Tejeda de Tiétar is a peaceful gateway to the La Vera region, surrounded by holm oaks, oak trees, and olive groves, overlooking the Tiétar River. Its historic center, with its whitewashed houses, holds treasures such as a Renaissance church and ancient Roman remains.

This viewpoint in Los Pueblos offers an incredible panoramic view. From here, you can see the towns of Tejeda de Tiétar, Arroyomolinos de la Vera, and Pasarón de la Vera, nestled in a beautiful meadow dotted with holm oaks and large oak trees. In the background, the incredible Gredos and Tormantos mountain ranges loom.
